summary: Validated legacy paged-pool size control. Microsoft Q&A captures PagedPoolSize = 0 as the default value, and the app writes that same reset value.

setting.name: Paged Pool Size
setting.casual_explanation: Paged pool is kernel memory that Windows can page out when needed. This is a legacy low-level memory tuning area that most people should never touch manually.
setting.why_people_change_it: People only change it when undoing old memory-management tweaks or investigating unusual low-pool conditions on older systems.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: The current app writes the documented default reset value, but this remains a low-level legacy tuning area.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft Q&A now captures PagedPoolSize = 0 as part of the default Memory Management registry snapshot. The current app writes PagedPoolSize = 0 under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Memory Management, matching that default reset value.

allowed_values.label: Default paged pool size
allowed_values.meaning: The default reset value captured in Microsoft Q&A.

allowed_values.label: Maximum paged pool for a specific older server issue
allowed_values.meaning: Older Microsoft troubleshooting guidance uses 0xFFFFFFFF to allocate the maximum paged pool for a specific problem scenario.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes PagedPoolSize = 0, matching the default value captured in Microsoft Q&A.

writes.notes: Observed app value now matches the documented default.

windows_defaults.label: Conservative publication baseline
windows_defaults.applies_to: Modern systems where this dataset has not yet published a validated PagedPoolSize reset mapping

states.rationale: Microsoft Q&A captures PagedPoolSize = 0 as the default value.

windows_defaults.notes: This record treats the default reset value as validated from Microsoft Q&A and documents the legacy tuning as low-level research, not a modern performance tweak.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
avoid_for: Research comparisons against the current app write

states.rationale: Keep the documented default reset value.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the documented default paged-pool behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You are trying to document the current app implementation as a non-default tweak.
tradeoffs: The current app write matches the documented default reset.

evidence.title: Microsoft Q&A: default registry value for Memory Management
evidence.summary: Microsoft Q&A captures PagedPoolSize = 0 as part of the default Memory Management registry snapshot.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Memory Management Registry Keys
evidence.summary: Microsoft's current published memory-management article documents PagedPoolLimit for 32-bit systems, but not PagedPoolSize as a current modern limit surface.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Unable to allocate memory from the system paged pool
evidence.summary: Microsoft's older troubleshooting guidance documents PagedPoolSize = 0xFFFFFFFF as a specific workaround for a server-side paged-pool exhaustion issue.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes PagedPoolSize = 0 for the current tweak.

decision.why: Microsoft Q&A now captures PagedPoolSize = 0 as part of the default Memory Management registry snapshot. The record matches the app and is validated for research, but it remains a low-level legacy tuning rather than a general Windows performance tweak.

validation_proof.notes: Microsoft Q&A captures the default Memory Management registry snapshot and includes PagedPoolSize = 0.
